label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out what date was a month ago from today, which is 9/7 (September 7th). The year in question is 2003 because Jane is watching the NFL 2003. So, the task is to find the date exactly one month prior to September 7, 2003. First, subtracting a month from September would take us to August. So the month part would be August, which is 08 in MM format. The day is 7th. So at first glance, subtracting a month would be 8/7/2003. But let me check the options. Option F is 08/07/2003. But wait, are there any complications here? For example, does August have 31 days? September has 30 days, but when moving back a month, if the original day is the 7th, and August has 31 days, then 7th is a valid date. So August 7th is valid. So one month ago from September 7th is August 7th. That would be 08/07/2003, which is option F. However, looking at the options given, option F is indeed present. But let me check if there's any trick here. Sometimes people might think of a "month ago" as 30 days, but that's not accurate because months vary in length. However, in this case, moving back one month from September 7th would just be August 7th, regardless of the number of days in the month. So the correct answer should be August 7th, 2003. So option F. But let me check the options again to make sure. The options are: (A) 08/14/2003 (B) 08/10/2003 (C) 08/08/2003 (D) 06/01/2003 (E) 04/07/2003 (F) 08/07/2003 Yes, option F is 08/07/2003. But wait, why is option E there as 04/07/2003? That would be April 7th,
